hermes.goal: Beat buy-and-hold (positive alpha vs BTC) while keeping max drawdown under 15% and never risking the mandate on a single trade — capital preservation first. Near-term: prove a sustained, fee-aware, positive out-of-sample edge on the paper trainer over ~90 days before scaling any live capital; grow the live account only on demonstrated alpha.

brain.commentary: The basket is dominated by high‑beta crypto assets with modest to negative composites and elevated volatilities (≈0.4‑0.55). Signals are largely flat with only a marginal long on SOL that rests on a tiny positive composite and negative alpha. Overall edge is weak, so we adopt a defensive stance and scale the portfolio down. SOL and LTC appear the most fragile – SOL because its long bias is barely justified, LTC because its composite and alpha are strongly negative while conviction is low.

counsellors.openai: Overall posture is **cautious/neutral**: three flat calls and only a marginal SOL long, while volatility (39–54%) and beta (0.89–1.24) imply meaningful downside sensitivity. Without position sizes, portfolio concentration cannot be judged; avoid interpreting “flat” as low risk.
